verses.1: A Maskil of Asaph. God, why have You rejected us forever? Why does Your anger smoke against the sheep of Your pasture?
verses.2: Remember Your congregation, which You purchased of old, Which You have redeemed to be the tribe of Your inheritance; And this Mount Zion, where You have dwelt.
verses.3: Step toward the irreparable ruins; The enemy has damaged everything in the sanctuary.
verses.4: Your adversaries have roared in the midst of Your meeting place; They have set up their own signs as signs.
verses.5: It seems like one bringing up His axe into a forest of trees.
verses.6: And now they break down all its carved work With axe and hammers.
verses.7: They have burned Your sanctuary to the ground; They have defiled the dwelling place of Your name.
verses.8: They said in their heart, “Let’s completely subdue them.” They have burned all the meeting places of God in the land.
verses.9: We do not see our signs; There is no longer any prophet, Nor is there anyone among us who knows how long.
verses.10: How long, God, will the enemy taunt You? Shall the enemy treat Your name disrespectfully forever?
verses.11: Why do You withdraw Your hand, even Your right hand? Extend it from Your chest and destroy them!
verses.12: Yet God is my King from long ago, Who performs acts of salvation in the midst of the earth.
verses.13: You divided the sea by Your strength; You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.
verses.14: You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
verses.15: You broke open springs and torrents; You dried up ever-flowing streams.
verses.16: Yours is the day, Yours also is the night; You have prepared the light and the sun.
verses.17: You have established all the boundaries of the earth; You have created summer and winter.
verses.18: Remember this, LORD, that the enemy has taunted You, And a foolish people has treated Your name disrespectfully.
verses.19: Do not give the soul of Your turtledove to the wild animal; Do not forget the life of Your afflicted forever.
verses.20: Consider the covenant; For the dark places of the land are full of the places of violence.
verses.21: May the oppressed person not return dishonored; May the afflicted and the needy praise Your name.
verses.22: Arise, God, and plead Your own cause; Remember how the foolish person taunts You all day long.
verses.23: Do not forget the voice of Your adversaries, The uproar of those who rise against You, which ascends continually.
